mappings.rationaleAI: Programmable machines executing operations via stored programs correspond to computers (D), types including digital and analog (D.1), programming systems (D.2), computer languages (D.3), and applications (D.4).
Devices converting sound into electronic signals for transmission correspond to telephone and telecommunications systems and equipment (I.4).
Transmission of information over distance via electrical or electronic means corresponds to major systems of communication (I), including telegraph (I.3), telephone (I.4), and radio (I.5).
One-to-many distribution of audio/audiovisual content via radio waves corresponds to radio communications (I.5) and television communications (I.6).
Design and operation of devices using transistors, diodes, and integrated circuits corresponds to electronic components (K), active components (K.1.a), and integrated circuits (K.1.c).
Instrument capturing images by focusing light corresponds to still photography technology (G.1).
Systems performing tasks associated with human intelligence — learning, reasoning, perception, and natural language processing — correspond to developments in artificial intelligence (D.5).
Portable wireless telephones operating over cellular networks correspond to telephone and telecommunications systems (I.4).
Founded theoretical computer science via the Turing machine (D), proposed the Turing test for AI (D.5), and led cryptanalysis of the Enigma cipher relevant to encryption and decryption (I.9).
Abstract concept of meaning transmission quantified in bits corresponds to communication and information theory (A) and information processing and systems (H).

Organized collections of physical and digital materials with catalogs correspond to organization and retrieval including computerized catalogs in libraries (H.1.c) and educational and reference systems (H.2.c).
Mass reproduction of text and images from woodblock printing through digital printing corresponds to printing machines and processes (E.3) and production of printing plates (E.4).
Telecommunication medium transmitting moving images corresponds to television communications (I.6), magnetic video recording (F.2), and optical video systems (F.3).
Mathematical practice of securing communications via encryption and authentication corresponds to encryption and decryption techniques and history of cryptology (I.9).
Radio wave detection for ranging and tracking corresponds to radar systems and equipment (J.1).
Capture of images by recording light corresponds to the technology of photography (G) and still photography (G.1).
Information about current events disseminated through print, broadcasting, and digital media corresponds to book, newspaper, and magazine publishing (I.1).
Making content publicly available in print or digital form corresponds to book, newspaper, and magazine publishing (I.1).
Written works distributed in printed, audio, or electronic form correspond to book publishing (I.1).
Global network-of-networks using TCP/IP corresponds to electronic networks (I.8).
Miniaturized assemblies of transistors and other components fabricated on semiconductor substrates correspond to integrated circuits (K.1.c).
Laser applications in optical disc storage and fiber-optic communications connect to holography and laser photography (G.3).
Invented the phonograph for sound recording, corresponding to mechanical systems for sound recording (F.1).
Invented the European movable-type printing press, corresponding to printing machines and processes (E.3) and production of printing plates (E.4).
Design and operation of robots integrating AI corresponds to developments in artificial intelligence including robotics (D.5).
Use of electromagnetic waves to transmit information wirelessly corresponds to radio communications systems and equipment (I.5).
Transmission and creation of meaning between senders and receivers corresponds to communication and information theory (A) and major systems of communication (I).
The shift to information-technology-centered economies via transistors and the internet corresponds to applications of computers (D.4) and electronic networks (I.8).
Electronic interactive systems played on console, computer, and mobile platforms correspond to applications of computers (D.4).
